annotations.label: Computer algebra system

anchor.section: (Lead)
anchor.snippet: is any mathematical software with the ability to manipulate mathematical expressions

annotations.note: Definition of a software system; not a mathematical statement formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classes of CAS

anchor.section: (Lead)
anchor.snippet: may be divided into two classes: specialized and general-purpose

annotations.note: Taxonomy of software systems; out of scope for Mathlib.

annotations.label: General-purpose CAS features

anchor.section: (Lead)
anchor.snippet: a general-purpose computer algebra system must include various features

annotations.note: Software feature checklist; not a Mathlib-formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Polynomial GCD in simplifier

anchor.section: (Lead)
anchor.snippet: the computation of polynomial greatest common divisors is systematically used for the simplification of expressions involving fractions

annotations.note: Polynomial GCD is defined in Mathlib via EuclideanDomain/Polynomial.gcd, though it is not used for fraction simplification.

annotations.label: Significant general-purpose systems

anchor.section: (Lead)
anchor.snippet: Significant systems include Axiom

annotations.note: List of CAS products; not mathematical content.

annotations.label: Schoonschip

anchor.section: History
anchor.snippet: designed a program for symbolic mathematics, especially high-energy physics, called Schoonschip

annotations.note: Historical software fact; out of scope for Mathlib.

annotations.label: MATHLAB

anchor.section: History
anchor.snippet: Carl Engelman created MATHLAB in 1964 at MITRE

annotations.note: Historical software fact; not formalizable in Mathlib.

annotations.label: HP-28 hand-held CAS

anchor.section: History
anchor.snippet: Hewlett-Packard introduced the first hand-held calculator CAS with the HP-28 series

annotations.note: Historical hardware fact; out of scope for Mathlib.

annotations.label: Early popular CAS

anchor.section: History
anchor.snippet: The first popular computer algebra systems were muMATH

annotations.note: Historical software fact; out of scope for Mathlib.

annotations.label: WolframAlpha

anchor.section: History
anchor.snippet: the release of WolframAlpha , an online search engine and CAS

annotations.note: Historical software fact; out of scope for Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symbolic manipulations supported

anchor.section: Symbolic manipulations
anchor.snippet: The symbolic manipulations supported typically include

annotations.note: List of CAS capabilities; not a single mathematical statement to formalize.

annotations.label: Additional CAS capabilities

anchor.section: Additional capabilities
anchor.snippet: Many also include: a programming language

annotations.note: List of software features; not a Mathlib-formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Types of expressions in a CAS

anchor.section: Types of expressions
anchor.snippet: The expressions manipulated by a CAS typically include multivariable polynomials

annotations.note: Describes CAS data types, not a Mathlib mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Numeric domains supported

anchor.section: Types of expressions
anchor.snippet: Numeric domains supported typically include floating-point representation of real numbers

annotations.note: Describes CAS numeric backends; not a mathematical theorem.

annotations.label: Knuth–Bendix completion

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Knuth–Bendix completion algorithm

annotations.note: Grep for 'KnuthBendix' in Mathlib returned no results.

annotations.label: Root-finding algorithms

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Root-finding algorithms

annotations.note: Mathlib has Hensel's lemma as a root-finding theorem but no general numerical root-finding algorithm.

annotations.label: Symbolic integration

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Symbolic integration via e.g. Risch algorithm or Risch–Norman algorithm

annotations.note: Grep for 'Risch' in Mathlib found nothing.

annotations.label: Hypergeometric summation

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Hypergeometric summation via e.g. Gosper's algorithm

annotations.note: Gosper's algorithm not in Mathlib; only hypergeometric series definitions exist.

annotations.label: Limit computation

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Limit computation via e.g. Gruntz's algorithm

annotations.note: Gruntz's algorithm for symbolic limits is not implemented in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polynomial factorization

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Polynomial factorization via e.g., over finite fields

annotations.note: Mathlib has existence/uniqueness of polynomial factorizations via UFM API but no algorithmic factorization routine.

annotations.label: Greatest common divisor

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Greatest common divisor via e.g. Euclidean algorithm

annotations.note: GCD via the Euclidean algorithm is formalized for Nat, Int, and any EuclideanDomain.

annotations.label: Gaussian elimination

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Gaussian elimination

annotations.note: Mathlib has matrix theory but no explicit Gaussian elimination algorithm/decl.

annotations.label: Gröbner basis

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Gröbner basis via e.g. Buchberger's algorithm

annotations.note: Monomial orderings exist in Mathlib but Buchberger's algorithm and Gröbner bases are not formalized.

annotations.label: Padé approximant

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Padé approximant

annotations.note: Grep for 'Padé/Pade' returned no Mathlib hits.

annotations.label: Schwartz–Zippel lemma

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Schwartz–Zippel lemma and testing polynomial identities

annotations.note: The Schwartz–Zippel lemma is formalized for multivariate polynomials in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chinese remainder theorem

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Chinese remainder theorem

annotations.note: CRT is formalized in Mathlib as Nat.chineseRemainder and ZMod.chineseRemainder, plus an ideal-theoretic version.

annotations.label: Diophantine equations

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Diophantine equations

annotations.note: Mathlib has Pell/Matiyasevic for Diophantine sets and specific Diophantine equations, but no general decision procedure.

annotations.label: Landau's algorithm

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Landau's algorithm (nested radicals)

annotations.note: Landau's nested-radical denesting algorithm is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Derivatives of elementary and special functions

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Derivatives of elementary functions and special functions

annotations.note: Mathlib has extensive derivative rules for elementary (exp, log, trig) and many special functions.

annotations.label: Cylindrical algebraic decomposition

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Cylindrical algebraic decomposition

annotations.note: CAD is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quantifier elimination over reals

anchor.section: Mathematics used in computer algebra systems
anchor.snippet: Quantifier elimination over real numbers via cylindrical algebraic decomposition

annotations.note: Mathlib has Presburger arithmetic quantifier elimination but not the Tarski–Seidenberg theorem for real-closed fields via CAD.
